steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The first sentence makes a general claim (the station "does much to serve" the community), and the second sentence provides specific examples (broadcasting news, music, and language programming). This is a specification pattern: a general claim followed by supporting details.

steps.title: Match the transition to the pattern
steps.content:
The logical relationship is specification: sentence 2 narrows and details sentence 1.
  • Choice A — Instead,: signals substitution or contradiction. The broadcast activities do not replace or oppose the prior claim. ✗
  • Choice B — Nevertheless,: signals a concession despite prior conditions. No prior obstacle is mentioned. ✗
  • Choice C — Specifically,: signals that the next clause details or narrows the preceding general claim. The list of broadcasts specifies what "does much" means. ✓
  • Choice D — As a result,: signals cause-effect. The broadcasts are not a consequence of serving the community; they are the means of doing so. ✗
C is correct.
